text: I have had mixed interactions at this Wegmans since I moved here in March 2026. I have been going in fairly regularly and buying croissants to make sandwiches on. Today I did so at lunch time. The croissant I ate was just on the edge of being too stale to eat. I checked the box and they had a sell by date of 5/6/26. Needless to say I threw them out. Tomorrow they will be too stale to eat. I paid $8 for FOUR croissants and as I left the store had boxes of 10 croissants for $10. Over priced and not worth it. I'm not sure this is going to be a store to buy anything but a boxed good in that is manufactured by someone other than Wegmans.

text:
UPDATE: visiting from out of town. It's still Wegmans.  Not sorry - I like this store.

2023- Never have I ever been to a store that sells groceries which is so HUGE! Covered parking garage with an escalator to the 2nd floor. No worries about toting your groceries. A very vast array of beers and wine. A Burger Bar which is really a restaurant.  Pizza made to order. Sandwiches, Desserts, Sushi, Deli, Wing Bar,  Seafood, Sushi bar. Flower area is nice and big. Of course fruits and vegetables. An international food aisle. And regular food also. Clean, neat store. 👌 we bought what we needed. Lots of check out stations and cashiers.

text:
Update April 2026: Been a little over a year since coming here and decided to give another chance.  Nothing has changed, gotten worse with stock, entire sections missing stock. Fresh steak and seafood was almost non-existent and when asked they said "We've haven't been getting our orders more often".  Glad the employee was honest about it.

I've been to this Wegmans dozens of times and many other locations they have. Wegmans has always been my #1 go to for grocery stores.  This one in VA Beach started out like the rest, great.  I won't go to this Wegmans or any other one again, this location ruined their name.  From the constant out of stock on multiple everyday items to pallets sitting in multiple isles every single time you go in.  It's not just 1 thing, it's a slew at this store and they are failing in every single aspect.  Kroger & Publix I'll stick with.

text: I love wegmans but here's why I gave it a 3 star review. Some of the things are over priced, but I mean where isn't right? I love the deli they have I come in a lot on my lunch breaks and it's became a weekly regimen for me. BUT sometimes I have to pick up catering orders for my job and I have to pick up before the actual deli/ bakery opens. More times than not the same older guy is working and almost always has to talk to me and every single time he has a poor attitude for no reason. My first encounter I was standing in the line that said pick up to pick up my order. He rudely says WERE CLOSED! NOT IOEN! I said my order said it was ready for pick up. He scrunched his face, rolled his eyes and pointed down the line, and pointed down the line. And whispered Jesus Christ under his breath, so I turned around and told him I'm so sorry I just assumed I'd be picking up at the pick up window. Another time, same man, gave me a catered order and slammed it on the serving thing, shoved it towards me and said HERE and walked off.

text:
Wegmans in Virginia Beach is simply exceptional. From the moment you step inside, the store feels bright, clean, and thoughtfully organized, making every visit enjoyable. The variety is impressive—fresh produce, specialty cheeses, bakery treats, and ready-to-eat meals are all beautifully presented and consistently high quality.
The staff truly set this location apart: friendly, attentive, and genuinely eager to help, which makes you feel appreciated as a customer. The prepared foods section alone is a standout—fresh, flavorful, and perfect when you want something quick without sacrificing quality.
With fair pricing, wide aisles, and fast, efficient checkout, shopping here is always smooth and stress-free. This store perfectly represents why Wegmans has such a stellar reputation nationwide.
Highly recommended for anyone in the Virginia Beach area! 🛒✨

text:
******
Jan 2026: snowstorm coming and I haven’t seen the large Wegmans parking garages packed! I had the same idea of sticking up my fave things: milk, Kobe beef brisket cold cuts $19.99 per lb but soooooo good so worth it! Best warm store-baked bread with it: cranberry orange bread. Still warm from the oven (with such a warm friendly smile from the bakery staff diligently slicing and bagging the bread!) In case I get snowed in, got myself dark chocolate hummus, great for my sweets craving without the guilt.
LOVE Wegmans: such unique offerings.
*****
Always get an experience for the senses when I shop at Wegman’s. Aisles are wide and well lit. And there are so many unique items to buy compared to other grocery stores: chocolate dipped figs from Spain, 2 million different kinds of cheese, salad and Asian bars, different prepared bowls and sushi, wide array of seafood and meats, bakery goods ranging from cookies to cakes to donuts and bagels. And oh don’t forget the various flavors of freshly made cream cheese like: garlic Parmesan, mixed berries, honey pecan, scallion vegetable, etc. and even different flavored hummus! Service here is great. The cashiers patiently explain to you how digital coupons work. Sushi chef nicely prepares you a shrimp tempura roll just because you wanted one. And I highly recommend getting a caramel macchiato from their cafe - better than Starbucks for sure!

text: Great store with many high-quality items.  A bit on the expensive side, but you can find some nice deals. Wegmans' store brand is excellent.  Check out their ice cream. Although their seafood is expensive, I love their meat section.  It's so lovely to get meat that's been trimmed and featherless.  The dessert selection and breads are delicious but pricey.  The produce is always fresh.  Try them out.  Update 1/2026.  Food quality has changed.  Subs meat quality has changed, bakery items taste has changed, along with portions and a price increase.  The produce is okay, but the quality is hit-or-miss. Wegmans brand foods have not changed and are a great buy.

text: Wegmans is a great store overall, and I truly enjoy shopping here. Every time I walk in, it feels like I’m back home — the atmosphere, quality, and customer service are consistently excellent. That said, I’m very disappointed by the lack of Buffalo Bills merchandise and themed items, especially considering Wegmans’ strong ties to Buffalo. The store rarely, if ever, has ready-to-go Buffalo Bills cakes, bakery toppers, or in-store displays. When you special order a Buffalo Bills cake, the customization is extremely limited — usually just wording with red, white, and blue sprinkles, which feels underwhelming for such a passionate fan base. Virginia actually has a large Buffalo Bills following, and it would be great to see that reflected more in the store and bakery. I really wish Wegmans leaned into the “Go Bills!” spirit a bit more. Despite this, I still love shopping here and appreciate everything Wegmans does well. I just hope to see more Buffalo pride represented in the future.

text: I Love this super market chain.  I always stop at them when traveling and when going to the Bills Game in Buffalo..I live in Hampton and was so excited when this location opened.  But during the football season my family has a tradition of Wegmans wings.  We always go before the game starts.  The locations in Buffalo always have the wing bar open before the games.  Since the NFL always has games on Sunday it makes absolutely no sense to not have the wing bar filled and ready for Sunday games.  I know they order and cook but I have to order at least 20 and then only able to do two different flavors? This becomes very expensive to get..More then two flavors.  I was truly disappointed. MAYBE NEXT SEASON THIS LOCATION WILL CHANGE THERE POLICY FOR FOOTBALL SEASON! Otherwise next season I will be making a new tradition and make them ourselves..

text: Wegmans used to be a favorite but since we have had to buy paper bags we started to bag our own. The problem is that they do not provide enough space to bag ourselves. If you don't bag correctly on that shelf The bag will fall off. We always feel super rushed now when we come into Wegmans because of the bagging process and we are old. Consequently we do most of our shopping at other stores now, whereas we used to do most of our shopping at Wegmans.

text: I love WEGMANS! I drive out of the way just to come here to do my groceries. Wegman's is so much better than Whole Foods in every way. I realized the prices on the fresh fruits and vegetables are much better even significantly lower while also being of higher quality. They have a great selection of organic and special dietary needs food options. I love the food stations. It's nice to get a slice of pizza or a sandwich made and can continue doing your shopping all in one place. All the bakery items are excellent. I always get a raspberry filled donut before I go. The only thing I wish they had was organic eggs like Vital Farms and Organic Chicken options like Bell & Evans. Other than that, everything else here is the best.

text:
I must say, Wegmans has it all. I don’t usually review food markets but this one is exceptional. Besides the typical groceries you’d find at your usual supermarket, Wegmans offers so much more. They have a dedicated aisle for gluten free shoppers, vitamins, protein supplements, etc… they have a very large bakery department with pretty much everything you’re shopping for. The produce department is stocked full of fresh vegetables and fruit.
Wegmans also has the largest dining/seating area of any market I’ve been to where you can purchase food, beverages, get a hot cup of coffee or cappuccino and sit down to enjoy it.
Need a bottle of wine or other spirits, they have you covered. There is an attached liquor store.

text:
Great sanitizing wipes near the carts and plenty of paper bags. Downside, not a lot of distancing and when I went on a weekend afternoon, much of the produce and fruit were gone, not as fresh as the morning stock. All the basic Wegman generic items like cheese and sauces were good. And while no train around their candy section, still covers the basics well.  Lots of great generic house brand items. And they have some made-near the store goods now, too, like pies and cakes. Still some of the best quality vs price metrics for me and some things like the produce and fruit are generally better. At this point in 2024, they do sometimes have samples.

Most of organic meats are really fresh. Sometimes get bad pork hocks in the regular section, not sure if it's a packing issue. Still great organic options. I wish they made the cranberry raisin bread more often.

The meat section has just been reorganized, before all the organic stuff was together but now it's split up by meat. Ground beef in a different case than the beef, it's off with the pork.

They also have great seasonal breads like the red white and blue bread right now which is very tasty. Right next door is a wine and liquor shop with all the basics and reasonable prices, and the hot foods section is busy at lunch.

Wings section is hit and miss - a little cheaper than other locations  but today's wings were super salty.

text: This Wegmans used to be my favorite grocery, however it has fallen off a cliff. The prices across the board have skyrocketed. I recently threw out $30 in fruits including a bag of Granny Smith apples at $8. Only one was not bruised or rotting. A $10 watermelon that rotted within 2 days days and was complete mush, and a bag of mangoes that didn’t last 3 days before rotting. text:
What was once my go-to place for groceries has turned into depressing corporate greed, lack of customer service and an all around worse shopping experience. Warning to others; if you use online shopping/delivery you will be upcharged 10-20% on every item. Do not be fooled by “no charge service fee”, they’re digging in your pockets with one hand and giving you a thumbs up with the other.
If your delivery shopper chooses to not pickup half of your order, they’ll happily refund you a $3 service fee, but nothing more. Be warned for those with disabilities who rely on delivery services, they give 10% effort at best.
You’ve turned a disciple into an adversary.
Still giving 2 stars because their blueberry K Cups are undeniably good, I’m a man of principle.

text:
⭐️ (1 out of 5 stars)
I visited this Wegmans in Parsippany for the first time in October 2025. The store looks beautiful and well-organized, but I was a bit disappointed with the snack selection — they didn’t have the most common chips flavors like sour cream and onion, which are usually easy to find everywhere.

Also, the beer section was already closed when I came around 11 p.m. I just wanted to buy a non-alcoholic brew, but since it’s located in the alcohol section, it was unavailable. That’s quite inconvenient. Other Wegmans locations are much better — they usually have a wider selection of chips and allow customers to buy non-alcoholic beverages until closing time.

text:
I had the absolute pleasure of working with Joe and Amy at Wegmans Floral, and I can’t say enough good things about them. I came in last minute, completely overwhelmed, needing a bouquet for my best friend’s wedding—and they went above and beyond.

Joe and Amy didn’t just throw something together; they took the time to listen to what I was envisioning, made thoughtful suggestions, and crafted the most gorgeous bouquet that looked like it had been planned for weeks. Their kindness, creativity, and calm energy made a stressful situation feel easy and even fun.

My best friend was blown away by how beautiful the flowers turned out, and I’ll never forget how Joe and Amy came through for such an important moment. Thank you both—you are floral magicians and lifesavers!
